channel.title: AI Builder's Field Guide — Recently Added
channel.description: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

item.title: CodeWhale
item.description: CodeWhale is a terminal-based AI coding agent designed for DeepSeek models, engineered for prefix-cache stability. — It provides a specialized coding agent optimized for DeepSeek models, offering a stable terminal experience for developers.

item.title: Open Design
item.description: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design, offering skills and design systems for generating various prototypes and media. — It provides an open-source, local alternative for AI-driven design generation, expanding options beyond proprietary tools like Claude Design.

item.title: CrewAI
item.description: CrewAI is a framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents, designed to simplify multi-agent teamwork and collaborative intelligence. — Unlike single-agent frameworks, CrewAI focuses on enabling seamless coordination among multiple agents to tackle complex tasks, streamlining multi-agent system development.

item.title: Dify
item.description: Dify is a low-code platform offering an all-in-one toolchain for building, deploying, and managing AI applications with agentic and retrieval-augmented workflows. — It provides a visual workflow builder to orchestrate tool-using agents, distinguishing it from code-centric agent frameworks by simplifying AI application development.

item.title: OpenHands
item.description: An open-source platform for autonomous AI software development agents capable of modifying code, running commands, and browsing the web. — It provides an open-source option for builders seeking autonomous coding agents, offering capabilities similar to proprietary solutions like Devin.

item.title: Hermes Agent
item.description: Hermes Agent is an open-source framework for building self-improving AI agents designed for long-term, personalized collaboration. — It emphasizes reliability and self-evolving skills, allowing agents to learn from feedback and adapt over time, contrasting with agents requiring constant manual refinement.

item.title: Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: The Microsoft Agent Framework is an open, multi-language framework for building and orchestrating production-grade AI agents and multi-agent workflows. — It provides a consistent foundation for agent systems across Python and .NET, supporting various LLM ecosystems for developers taking agents from prototype to production.

item.title: Helicone
item.description: Helicone provides logging, monitoring, and debugging for large language model applications to help builders understand and optimize their AI usage. — It offers detailed insights into LLM calls, including latency and cost, providing a more focused observability solution than broader eval platforms.
